cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the text carefully and find the answer step by step. The question asks about what the films children watch at school actually do, according to the text. The relevant part of the text says: "In school they view motion pictures which are supposedly meant to teach them how to deal with arms safely but which are actually designed to stimulate a desire to own a gun." Let's break this down: 1. The films are "supposedly meant to teach them how to deal with arms safely" - this is what the films claim to do. 2. But "actually designed to stimulate a desire to own a gun" - this is the real purpose according to the author. Looking at the answer choices: (A) teach them how to deal with guns safely - This is what the films claim to do, but the text is critical of this and says it's not the actual purpose. (B) praise hunting as character-building - The text mentions that gunners describe their actions as character-building, but it doesn't specifically say that the films do this. (C) describe hunting as an exercise - This is not mentioned
